ksa_text: Knowledge of AI governance structures and framework components, including model risk management, AI lifecycle oversight, accountability assignment, AI system registries, tiered review processes, impact assessment methodologies, post-deployment monitoring requirements, and the organizational conditions that enable responsible AI deployment at scale.

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals are empowered, responsible, and trained for mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks.

top_anchors.element_label: Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals are empowered, responsible, and trained for mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks.
Capability building for digital, data and technology

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA AG-K-002 (AI Governance/Knowledge) excluded as out_of_scope (composite 0.579, highest: NIST AI RMF at 0.579). Domain prior: out. Core hits: 0. OOS hits: 0. Seniority: appropriate. IC-level data quality analysis: profiling, quality rule authoring, reporting, exception handling, and quality metrics measurement.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency. Exclude consistent with out_of_scope classification, composite 0.579, and AI Governance (AG) handling.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence challenge the exclusion of this AI Governance KSA for Data Quality Analyst?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: AI Governance competencies do not appear as Data Quality Analyst requirements in job postings. They are allocated to specialist roles. Exclusion consistent with market patterns.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Exclusion upheld by market evidence. AI Governance is not a Data Quality Analyst core competency. Final decision: exclude.
